title: JV-14 - TUI MT status, loading/error states, and 60fps frame-pacing evidence

labels.name: level: atomic
labels.description: Small independent execution slice that can be assigned directly

body:
## Summary

Surface local MT state in the TUI and integrate with the 60fps performance gate.

## Why is this needed?

Realtime translation must keep the terminal responsive while model load/errors/fallback route are visible to users.

## Current context

This WBS comes from tentacle research on 2026-05-21. Current repo state: mt_provider still defaults to google; src/bin/mt_bench.rs emits a pending lf-04-v1 fixture; local OPUS-MT provider exists behind local-mt but lacks benchmark-proven realtime replacement evidence; config has explicit mt_cloud_fallback consent; #372 closed with routing/fixture only; #383 tracks 60fps render performance; PR #407 aligns docs with local-first STT but does not make local MT default.

## Inputs

- Existing #383 60fps issue.
- src/main.rs render loop currently includes 50ms sleep.
- Runtime states from JV-11/JV-12.
- TUI status strip and settings UI.

## Outputs

- MT state/status labels: loading model, local direct, routed via Google, unsupported pair, timeout/error.
- Frame pacer or integration with #383 evidence.
- docs/evidence/dm-07-fps.md or JV-specific fps artifact referencing local MT.

## Implementation approach

- Do not block render loop on model load/inference.
- Use dirty flag/adaptive frame pacing if implementing within #383.
- Record baseline and branch p95 frame time in single and dual mode.
- Keep narrow terminal fallback readable.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Single-mode and dual-mode p95 frame time meet #383 thresholds or documented successor thresholds.
- [ ] MT state changes are visible without leaking text or key.
- [ ] User can distinguish local, Google fallback, missing model, and unsupported pair.
- [ ] No regression in keyboard controls.

## Test cases / evidence gates

- [ ] TUI snapshot/focused render tests for MT states.
- [ ] Frame-time benchmark artifact.
- [ ] PTY or headless TestBackend test.
- [ ] Soak confirms no UI stalls during model load.

## Dependencies

- Depends on JV-11/JV-12 for real states.
- Extends #383; can be implemented as part of or after #383.

## Agent routing

Build: TUI/performance specialist. Review: tui-rust-code-reviewer and nfr-verification-gate.

## Project / WBS

- WBS key: JV-14
- Wave: U1 UI/perf
- Project target: magicpro97/project/2
- Labels: type: feature, area: tui, area: performance, area: verification, priority:P1, level: atomic

## Duplicate / extension note

Extends #383 with MT-specific UI states; does not duplicate generic 60fps gate.
